Once a prospect replies to cold outreach, the thread moves from "cold" to "warm" — the agent's job shifts from opening a conversation to **qualifying**, **matching to the bench**, and **booking a discovery call** with a human context brief attached.

input.hiring_signal_brief_excerpt: The prospect, Sarah, is a data scientist. Your CRM shows a note from a previous rep saying, 'Sarah mentioned they are exploring new data visualization tools but seemed hesitant about committing to a specific solution.' The company website mentions 'AI-powered data insights' prominently.
input.competitor_gap_brief_excerpt: We're looking for something that can handle large datasets and offer flexible visualization options. We've tried a few things, but nothing has really clicked yet.
input.prior_thread: Sarah, thanks for connecting! I saw you were exploring data visualization. Our platform, DataGenius, offers advanced analytics. Would you be open to a quick chat to see if it aligns with your needs?
input.bench_context:
# Tenacious Style Guide

Five tone markers. Every outreach, every reply, every discovery-call context brief your agent produces must preserve these markers. A tone drift of more than two markers in a single message is a Tenacious brand violation and will be caught by the tone-preservation probe in A

candidate_output.subject: Question: DataGenius signal check
candidate_output.body:
Hi Sarah, I hope you're having a productive week.

Based on your interest in flexible data visualization for large datasets, I was wondering if you've had a chance to explore how AI-powered insights could enhance your current tools?

DataGenius specializes in this area, and I'd be happy to share a brief overview if that's something you're open to. Does your team have any specific challenges with current visualization tools?

If useful, I can route one bounded follow-up to our delivery lead.

input.hiring_signal_brief_excerpt: The prospect, Mark, is an IT Director. Your CRM shows a recent blog post from his company discussing 'the evolving landscape of cloud security challenges.' There's also an old note (6 months old) from a marketing webinar signup where Mark indicated interest in 'data encryption solutions.'
input.competitor_gap_brief_excerpt: Our current security stack is robust, but with the constant changes in cloud threats, we're always evaluating new approaches to stay ahead.
input.prior_thread: Mark, I noticed your company's blog on cloud security. CloudSecure offers advanced threat detection. Would you be interested in learning more about our encryption features?
input.bench_context:
# Tenacious Style Guide

Five tone markers. Every outreach, every reply, every discovery-call context brief your agent produces must preserve these markers. A tone drift of more than two markers in a single message is a Tenacious brand violation and will be caught by the tone-preservation probe in A

candidate_output.subject: Question: CloudSecure signal check
candidate_output.body:
Hi Mark, I hope your week is going well.

I saw your team's discussion on evolving cloud security challenges. Given your focus on staying ahead, I was curious if you're currently exploring solutions that integrate advanced threat detection with data encryption?

CloudSecure provides comprehensive tools in this space. Would you be open to a brief discussion to see if our approach aligns with your current evaluations? What are your primary concerns regarding new cloud threats?

If useful, I can route one bounded follow-up to our delivery lead.

Once a prospect replies to cold outreach, the thread moves from "cold" to "warm" — the agent's job shifts from opening a conversation to **qualifying**, **matching to the bench**, and **booking a discovery call** with a human context brief attached.
